understanding.project_summary: This project provides a developer-documentation engine (AutoDocX) that scans a local Python codebase, extracts structural metadata, generates an LLM-assisted narrative understanding, and produces standalone HTML developer documentation. It exposes a FastAPI HTTP API for scanning projects, generating and listing scans, producing LLM understandings, comparing scans, and creating or updating HTML documentation; a Streamlit UI client is included to call those APIs.
understanding.architecture_overview: The application is organized into clear layers: a FastAPI entry layer (app/main.py and app.api.project_routes) that exposes HTTP endpoints; service layers under app.services that implement scanning, parsing, analysis, LLM integration, comparison, runtime context analysis, and document building; storage layers (app.services.scan_storage, app.services.understanding_storage, app.services.document_storage) that persist scans, understanding JSON, and generated HTML plus runtime assets; and a small UI client (ui/streamlit_app). Configuration and workspace paths are centralized in app.core.config. Runtime moves from request -> ProjectScanner (uses PythonCodeParser and ProjectAnalyzer) to produce a scan -> ScanStorage saves the scan -> ProjectUnderstandingService (uses ProjectContextBuilder and LLM provider factory) generates narrative understanding -> UnderstandingStorage saves it -> DocumentBuilder renders HTML -> DocumentStorage saves HTML and any runtime assets. Additional flows support scan comparison, LLM runtime context analysis (vision-capable providers), and recovery of embedded assets from existing HTML files.

execution_flow.title: Start application and health check
execution_flow.description: Start the FastAPI app (app/main.py). The root (/) and /health endpoints return basic health/status responses used by the UI and orchestration.

execution_flow.title: Scan a local project
execution_flow.description: Client calls POST /api/projects/scan with a ProjectScanRequest. ProjectScanner validates the path, enumerates files, parses Python files via PythonCodeParser, analyzes project structure with ProjectAnalyzer, and returns a ProjectScanResponse. ScanStorage persists the stored scan under the configured workspace path.

execution_flow.title: Generate structured project understanding (LLM)
execution_flow.description: Client calls POST /api/projects/{project_name}/understand. The endpoint loads a stored scan, builds an LLM-ready project context using ProjectContextBuilder, selects and constructs an LLM provider via LLMProviderFactory (OpenAIProvider or AnthropicProvider), sends prompts using provider.generate_json, parses the JSON response, merges deterministic parser/analyzer reference data, validates required fields, and saves the result with UnderstandingStorage.

execution_flow.title: Build HTML documentation
execution_flow.description: Client calls POST /api/projects/{project_name}/documents to generate HTML. The handler retrieves a stored understanding, DocumentBuilder.build_html renders a professional standalone HTML page (with navigation, API reference cards, signatures, etc.), and DocumentStorage.save_document writes the HTML and JSON metadata to the documents workspace. DocumentStorage.prepare_runtime_assets handles uploaded screenshots when provided.

execution_flow.title: Sync complete documentation (all-in-one)
execution_flow.description: POST /api/projects/documentation/sync triggers the full pipeline: scan_project -> save scan -> locate latest document -> create initial documentation or compare scans and create new version when changes exist -> return current document metadata. This endpoint centralizes the scan->understand->document workflow.

execution_flow.title: Sync with runtime context and screenshots
execution_flow.description: POST /api/projects/documentation/sync-with-context accepts form fields and uploaded screenshots. Uploaded images are staged and handed to DocumentStorage.prepare_runtime_assets; a RuntimeContextAnalyzer can call a vision-capable LLM provider to analyze screenshots with code context. Returned runtime insights are merged into the final documentation.

execution_flow.title: Compare scans and list artifacts
execution_flow.description: The API supports comparing saved scans (POST /api/projects/{project_name}/compare) using ScanComparator.compare_scans, listing scans (/scans) and documents (/documents), and retrieving specific stored scan/understanding/document files and their metadata.

api_overview.purpose: API endpoint discovered from FastAPI route decorators.

risks_and_gaps.title: No authentication or access controls on API endpoints
risks_and_gaps.description: The API surface (project_routes) exposes scan, LLM calls, and document download endpoints without documented authentication or authorization controls in the scanned code. This may expose project source and LLM keys if deployed publicly.

risks_and_gaps.title: Secrets and credential management
risks_and_gaps.description: LLM API keys and provider selection are read from environment variables via app.core.config but there is no documented secret management, rotation, or vault integration in the scanned code.

risks_and_gaps.title: Observability and monitoring
risks_and_gaps.description: There are no explicit logging, metrics, or tracing integrations visible in the codebase scan that would support production observability, error monitoring, or performance metrics for long-running LLM calls.

risks_and_gaps.title: Upload and file size handling
risks_and_gaps.description: While ProjectScanner sets ignored extensions and size limits for scanning, the API paths accepting uploaded screenshots do not show explicit rate limiting or comprehensive validation beyond allowed extensions; large uploads and resource exhaustion are possible.

risks_and_gaps.title: Testing and CI coverage not exposed
risks_and_gaps.description: The scan does not include test modules or CI configuration; unit/integration tests and repeatable CI/CD scripts are not present in the provided files.

risks_and_gaps.title: Deployment and runtime documentation missing
risks_and_gaps.description: There are start/stop scripts and a requirements.txt, but no container manifests, process manager config, or deployment guidance for production (TLS, reverse proxy, scalability, or persistent storage configuration).

risks_and_gaps.title: Potential LLM response validation and retry behavior
risks_and_gaps.description: LLM provider implementations parse and validate JSON responses and ProjectUnderstandingService references retry helpers, but global retry/backoff strategies for transient failures and timeouts across all LLM and network calls are not documented as configurable.

recommended_document_sections.title: Quickstart: Running AutoDocX locally
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Describe prerequisites, environment variables (.env), workspace layout, and how to start the service using provided start scripts or a recommended Python/uvicorn command.

recommended_document_sections.title: API Reference and Example Requests
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Document each HTTP endpoint with request/response schemas, example curl/requests bodies, and sample responses for scan, understand, document generation, comparison and retrieval flows.

recommended_document_sections.title: Workspace and Storage Layout
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Explain SCANS_PATH, UNDERSTANDINGS_PATH, DOCUMENTS_PATH, how files are named and where attachments/screenshots are stored and recovered.

recommended_document_sections.title: LLM Configuration and Providers
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Document supported LLM providers, environment variables required for providers, model selection, and guidance on using vision-capable models for runtime screenshot analysis.

recommended_document_sections.title: Runtime/Tooling Context Workflow
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Explain the sync-with-context flow, how to prepare context blocks and screenshots, how DocumentStorage.prepare_runtime_assets works, and how runtime insights are merged into documentation.

recommended_document_sections.title: Security and Secrets Guide
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Provide instructions for securing the API (authentication), storing LLM API keys securely, and recommended network/security posture for production deployments.

recommended_document_sections.title: Observability and Production Hardening
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Recommendations and examples for adding logging, metrics, tracing, timeouts, retries, request size limits, and deployment patterns (containers, reverse proxy, TLS).

recommended_document_sections.title: Testing and CI Recommendations
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Describe suggested unit and integration tests around parsing, scanning, LLM integration (mocked), storage, and end-to-end sync flows and provide a sample CI pipeline.

recommended_document_sections.title: Extending Providers and Customization
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Guidance for adding new LLM providers, customizing prompts, adjusting ProjectContextBuilder modes, and adapting DocumentBuilder templates.

api_reference.summary: Create or update project documentation using one request
api_reference.docstring:
Complete documentation workflow.

The endpoint:

1. Scans and saves the project.
2. Finds the latest document.
3. Creates initial documentation when no
   document exists.
4. Compares scans when a document exists.
5. Creates a new version when changes exist.
6. Returns the existing document when there
   are no changes.

api_reference.docstring:
Complete documentation workflow with external
runtime/tooling context.

This endpoint keeps the original code documentation
pipeline but also accepts human-written context and
screenshots. The extra context is added to the final
HTML documentation under Runtime and Tooling Context.

api_reference.summary: Compare a new scan and create an updated documentation version
api_reference.docstring:
Update an existing generated document using a new
saved scan.

Important:
- If the previous document contains runtime/tooling
  context, it is carried into the new document.
- This prevents screenshots, notes, and runtime
  understanding from disappearing during manual
  document updates.
called_functions_preview: router.post('/{project_name}/documents/update', response_model=DocumentUpdateResponse, status_code=status.HTTP_200_OK, summary='Compare a new scan and create an updated documentation version')
